description: FOREMAN crew model. Vision perceives; the Supervisor orchestrates. EVERY case runs the always-on Diagnosis & Recommendation Engine (the brain: Skills + Context-Grounding reference docs + Data Fabric + Memory + Neo4j). The Supervisor then assembles ONLY the dynamic specialists whose invoke_when the findings satisfy - and can JUSTIFY why it skipped the others. 10 dynamic specialists.

always_on.name: Diagnosis & Recommendation Engine
always_on.role: The core brain - runs on EVERY case. Where FOREMAN's value ties together: match a learned Skill (hard gate), cite the reference PDFs, read the asset master record (Data Fabric), recall Agent Memory, reason to a root cause, emit the cited fix; on close write/strengthen the Skill.

dynamic_specialists.name: Fleet & Blast-Radius
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the asset shares a failure-propagating link with others - same batch, vendor, firmware, power feeder, cooling loop, or install crew
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: telecom cable batch, solar connector lot / install-crew, IMM resin lot, transformer feeder

dynamic_specialists.name: Warranty & Entitlement
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the asset is in a warranty window AND the root cause is a vendor / batch / spec / manufacturing defect (a claimable cost) - NOT for field-workmanship faults
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: cable spec defect, connector-lot defect, machine OEM defect, transformer supplier defect

dynamic_specialists.name: SLA & Commercial Impact
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the asset/site serves multiple tenants/customers/an SLA AND the commercial exposure could CHANGE the priority or action
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: tower tenants (multi-SLA), solar PPA / generation, factory line output, building occupants

dynamic_specialists.name: Safety & Compliance
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the matched skill has safety_protocol=true, OR the equipment/failure is hazardous (arc/fire, fall, derailment, pressure, HV)
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: PV DC arc (NEC 690.11), lift/escalator (EN 81 / A17.1), rail hot-box, HV transformer, pressure systems

dynamic_specialists.name: Parts & Logistics
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the recommended fix needs a replacement part or consumable
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: matched MC4 pair, NRV / check-ring kit, bimetallic screw, HV bushing, bearing, diaphragm

dynamic_specialists.name: Field Dispatch & Scheduling
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: a physical repair, swap or inspection visit is required
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: tower rigger, CERTIFIED PV technician, IMM service engineer, licensed lift inspector

dynamic_specialists.name: Telemetry & Predictive
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the fault is INCIPIENT / degrading and a sensor trend would decide fix-now-vs-schedule - NOT when the part has already hard-failed
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: RF VSWR/PIM, PV string current + IR thermal, cushion / vibration SPC, transformer DGA, bearing temp

dynamic_specialists.name: Vendor & Supply-Chain Liaison
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the root cause implicates a SUPPLIER - a batch/lot defect, a recall, counterfeit / substituted parts (procurement) - NOT a one-off field workmanship fault
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: cable batch recall, counterfeit connector lot, machine OEM bulletin, transformer supplier

dynamic_specialists.name: Site Access & Weather Window
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: the fix is outdoor / at height / in a live environment / weather-sensitive, or site access is constrained
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: solar (dry + daylight), tower climb (wind), rail (track possession), substation (permit-to-work)

dynamic_specialists.name: Cost Optimization (Repair vs Replace)
dynamic_specialists.invoke_when: there is a GENUINE repair-vs-replace-vs-upgrade trade-off - NOT when the fix is forced/trivial
dynamic_specialists.horizontal: rebuild vs replace a spindle, repair vs replace a transformer, patch vs re-cable a run

mc4_demo_invocation.scenario: Field engineer films a melted MC4 connector with frayed copper at the crimp (PV string).

invoked.why: DC arc + fire is safety-critical (NEC 690.11). Isolate under no-load + LOTO; BLOCKS auto-resolve - the human gate is mandatory. This is the non-negotiable one.
a poor-crimp / cross-mating root cause is a SYSTEMIC install defect, not a one-off. Audit the same installer's other strings and sites - one finding can mean dozens of latent fire risks.
the fix needs a MATCHED GENUINE MC4 pair + the correct crimp die. Confirm stock and lead time before a truck rolls.
physical re-termination is required, by a CERTIFIED PV technician - DC arc work is not for any hand.
outdoor DC electrical work needs a DRY weather window and adequate daylight; schedule into a safe window, not into rain.

not_invoked.why: the VISIBLE frayed copper at the crimp is a FIELD WORKMANSHIP defect, not a vendor spec/manufacturing defect - there is no claimable warranty path. Opening a claim would be wrong and waste a cycle. (Contrast the RF case, where a non-marine SPEC defect WAS claimable.)

no confirmed supplier defect or recall - workmanship is the lead cause. HELD for re-engagement only if the Fleet audit proves the failures are actually systemic cross-mating (a procurement substitution); don't escalate a supplier on a crew's bad crimp.

a single PV string is a small, bounded generation loss - and the action is ALREADY MANDATED by safety (fire risk). A commercial number cannot change a fire-risk decision, so computing one is wasted effort. (Contrast the RF tower: multi-tenant ~Rs 48k/hr DID drive the action.)

the connector has ALREADY HARD-FAILED - melted, live now. There is nothing incipient to trend or predict, and the string-current signal is already lost. The decision is immediate; a trend analysis would only add latency.

no repair-vs-replace trade-off exists - a charred connector is ALWAYS cut out and replaced; you never 'repair' a fire risk or run it to failure. Safety-optimal and cost-optimal are identical, so there is nothing to optimise.
